description:
Remove the execute permission bit of /etc/bin/sudo for the other users. To properly set the permissions of /usr/bin/sudo, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 4110 /usr/bin/sudo
rationale: Restricting the set of users able to execute commands as privileged user reduces the attack surface.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /usr/bin/sudo, run the command: 
$ ls -l /usr/bin/sudo
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: ---s--x---
title: Ensure only owner and members of group owner of /usr/bin/sudo can execute it
